on-success.action: Close GitHub issue #744 with VRAM measurement + run summary
Create PR test/mambavesselnet → main via gh pr create

on-failure.action: Create GitHub issue with diagnosis from outputs/ralph_diagnoses.jsonl
DO NOT hang — exit cleanly with structured error report

phase.name: Docker Build + Push
phase.description: Build Dockerfile.base with INSTALL_MAMBA=1 (CUDA-compiled mamba-ssm==2.3.1). This is the single most time-consuming step. If it fails, the entire plan stops cleanly — no cloud resources wasted.

task.name: Build :mamba-latest image
task.acceptance: Build exits 0. Verify: docker run --rm ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:mamba-latest \ python -c "import mamba_ssm; print(f'mamba_ssm {mamba_ssm.__version__} OK')" Must print: mamba_ssm 2.3.1 OK (or similar). Must NOT raise ImportError.
task.failure-action: STOP ENTIRE PLAN. Write error to /tmp/ralph-mamba-build-error.txt. Common causes: CUDA version mismatch, disk full, OOM during nvcc compilation. DO NOT attempt cloud launch without a valid image.

task.name: Push to GHCR
task.precondition: T1.1 succeeded AND GITHUB_TOKEN is set in .env
task.acceptance: docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:mamba-latest returns valid JSON manifest. Push timestamp within last 2 hours.
task.failure-action: STOP ENTIRE PLAN. Likely cause: GITHUB_TOKEN missing or expired. Check: grep GITHUB_TOKEN .env | head -1

task.name: Verify RunPod enabled + GPU available

commands.cmd: uv run sky check runpod 2>&1 | grep -i enabled
uv run sky gpus --cloud runpod 2>&1 | grep -E "RTX.?4090|RTX.?5090|RTX.?3090" | head -3

task.acceptance: RunPod: enabled. At least one GPU type available.

task.name: Verify Docker image in registry
task.failure-action: Image not pushed. Go back to P1.T1.2.

task.name: Verify smoke_test_mamba.yaml correctness

commands.cmd: python3 -c " import yaml from pathlib import Path cfg = yaml.safe_load(Path('deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_mamba.yaml').read_text(encoding='utf-8')) envs = cfg.get('envs', {}) assert envs.get('MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI') == '/opt/vol/mlruns', 'MLflow must be file-based on NV' assert 'DVC_S3_ENDPOINT_URL' not in envs, 'UpCloud vars must NOT be present' assert 'MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME' not in envs, 'UpCloud MLflow auth must NOT be present' vols = cfg.get('volumes', {}) assert '/opt/vol' in vols, 'Network Volume must be at /opt/vol' res = cfg.get('resources', {}) assert 'docker:' in str(res.get('image_id', '')), 'Must use Docker image_id' print('YAML pre-flight: ALL CHECKS PASSED') "

task.acceptance: ALL CHECKS PASSED. No UpCloud references.

task.name: Verify AMP policy (D04)

commands.cmd: python3 -c " import yaml from pathlib import Path p = Path('configs/experiment/smoke_mambavesselnet_cloud.yaml') if p.exists(): cfg = yaml.safe_load(p.read_text(encoding='utf-8')) assert cfg.get('mixed_precision') is True, 'train AMP must be ON' assert cfg.get('mixed_precision_val') is False, 'val AMP must be OFF (MONAI #4243)' print('AMP policy OK: train=ON, val=OFF') else: print('WARNING: smoke config not found — will use Hydra defaults') "

task.acceptance: AMP policy OK or config missing (Hydra defaults apply).

task.name: Launch smoke test
task.description:
Uses make smoke-test-mamba which runs: sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/smoke_test_mamba.yaml --env-file .env -y
Network Volume data strategy (handled in smoke_test_mamba.yaml setup block): 1. Check /opt/vol/data/raw/minivess/imagesTr/ — if populated, symlink 2. If empty: dvc pull from s3://minivessdataset (public, no credentials) 3. Cache pulled data on volume for future runs
No user intervention needed. The YAML handles both cases autonomously.
task.acceptance: Job ID returned. sky jobs queue shows PENDING or RUNNING.

task.name: Monitor to completion (30s poll)
task.description: Watch for: - "mamba_ssm X.X.X CUDA ready" — CUDA extension loaded - "VRAM_PEAK_MB=NNNN" — T09 measurement captured - Finite train_loss/val_loss values — training succeeded - Job status SUCCEEDED — clean exit

commands.cmd: JOB_ID=$(uv run sky jobs queue 2>&1 | grep mamba | head -1 | awk '{print $1}') uv run sky jobs logs $JOB_ID --no-follow 2>&1 | tee /tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt

task.acceptance: Job SUCCEEDED. Logs contain VRAM_PEAK_MB. Training losses finite.

phase.name: Diagnose + Fix
phase.description: On failure: classify, fix, relaunch. Max 3 cloud retries. CRITICAL: Max 1 Docker rebuild per session. If a second rebuild is needed, the issue is too complex for autonomous resolution — create GitHub issue and STOP.

pattern.fix: Check Dockerfile.base runner stage. Requires 1 Docker rebuild (45 min).
Reduce batch_size to 1 in experiment config. No rebuild needed.
Verify mixed_precision_val=false in config. No rebuild needed.
TDD: write test for failing shape, fix BidirectionalMambaLayer. Requires rebuild.

smoke_test_mamba.yaml setup already handles this: 1. Check Network Volume → symlink if present 2. If empty → dvc pull from s3://minivessdataset (public, no creds) If both fail: create GitHub issue (network/bucket problem).
SkyPilot auto-tries RTX4090 → RTX5090 → RTX3090 across regions.
Make GHCR package public: GitHub → Packages → minivess-base → Settings → Public.

constraints.on-budget-exceeded: STOP, create GitHub issue with diagnosis, exit cleanly
constraints.on-unknown-failure: Create GitHub issue, attach logs, exit cleanly

task.name: Extract VRAM from logs
task.description: VRAM_PEAK_MB is printed by the run: block in smoke_test_mamba.yaml. Extract from /tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt using str.partition (no regex).

commands.cmd: python3 -c " from pathlib import Path log = Path('/tmp/ralph-mamba-logs.txt').read_text(encoding='utf-8') for line in log.splitlines(): if 'VRAM_PEAK_MB=' in line: _, _, val = line.partition('VRAM_PEAK_MB=') mb = int(val.strip().split()[0]) print(f'T09 VRAM: {mb} MB ({mb / 1024:.1f} GB)') break else: print('WARNING: VRAM_PEAK_MB not found — check logs manually') "

task.name: Update model profile YAML
task.description: Write measured VRAM to configs/model_profiles/mambavesselnet.yaml. Update vram.measured: true, add rtx4090_patch64 value.
task.acceptance: make test-staging passes after update (or at minimum the mamba config tests).

task.name: Commit + close #744 + create PR
task.description: NOTE on MLflow sync: After sky jobs launch, worker pod is destroyed. MLflow artifacts are on Network Volume but inaccessible until next pod launch. For closing #744, VRAM measurement from logs is SUFFICIENT. MLflow sync (make dev-gpu-sync) can be done manually later.

commands.cmd:
git commit -m "feat(mambavesselnet): T09 VRAM measured on RTX 4090, closes #744
VRAM peak: [NNNN] MB @ patch=(64,64,64), batch=1 AMP: train=ON, val=OFF (D04, MONAI #4243) MLflow: file-based on Network Volume, sync later via make dev-gpu-sync
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>"

gh issue close 744 --comment "T09 VRAM measured + T10 smoke test PASSED on RunPod RTX 4090. VRAM peak: [NNNN] MB @ patch=(64,64,64), batch=1. MLflow: /opt/vol/mlruns (Network Volume). Sync: make dev-gpu-sync. Branch: test/mambavesselnet"

gh pr create --title "feat(mambavesselnet): T09-T10 VRAM measured + smoke test on RunPod" \ --body "## Summary - T09: VRAM peak measured on RTX 4090 @ patch=(64,64,64), batch=1 - T10: MambaVesselNet++ smoke test passed on RunPod (2 epochs, 1 fold, 4 vols) - MLflow: file-based on Network Volume, sync via make dev-gpu-sync - Closes #744
## Test plan - [x] Smoke test SUCCEEDED on RunPod RTX 4090 - [x] VRAM_PEAK_MB captured in logs - [x] val_loss finite (AMP val OFF, D04)
Generated with Claude Code (overnight autonomous run)"

task.acceptance: Issue #744 closed. PR created. Branch pushed to origin.

ralph-loop-parameters.on-unrecoverable: Create GitHub issue with diagnosis, attach logs, exit cleanly

close.reason: T09 VRAM measured + T10 smoke test PASSED on RunPod (Phase 5)
